item.title: Instant Karma
item.description: A teenager was hit with a pressurized hose at a car wash after throwing a drink at an employee who retaliated. The incident occurred at an Indiana car wash where 18-year-old Anna Harycki was on duty. "I picked up the pressure washer and started spraying down their car. The girl rolled down her window and threw lemonade at me, and I was in shock." Harycki said her immediate "reaction" was to just blast the driver with the water. The customer and her boyfriend were subsequently banned from the car wash for life. #instantkarma #instantregret #winstupidprizes
item.dc:creator: birdieboo2121

item.title: Moon light
item.description: Lighting up the room like the moon. LED light and cement.
item.dc:creator: funmaster2024

item.title: A BIG OLE' WHAT THE FRUCK AT WORK THIS WEEK...
item.description:
I'm laid back at work, avoid the limelight, don't need to be the star of the show, unlike some of my teammates.
This guy is so professional and well-grounded, he's so much better than this position. WTF is he doing here?
I've had little else to do for 6 months than comb through the written procedures and do improvements. No one asked me to so I haven't officially published anything, but this is my bread-and-butter so I'm confident in my work.
A friend recommended me to a job a his company, and they offered me a spot with a 20%+ raise. Duh, I said yes.
Ugh. So this other guy.... he's been actively trying to get me fired for months because he's all flash and carrot-chasing, and I just get shit done right in the sidelines and am actually useful, and after the initial awe wore off from his 'brilliance' I was getting more accolades, and for some reason he just decided to take that personally?
She isn't a peach herself, half the problem really, but at least this time she's doing the right thing.
Misogynist Guy and Mr. Racist get to meet with HR on Monday. For two hours. Each.
He's maybe more pissed off about the whole thing than I am, though he just started and it's been nothing but red flags from Day 1 for him. Our old manager quit right after hiring him, before he started even.
The stupid is unending, I'm shocked I could tolerate it this long.
What they did is... uhh.... an actual crime.... so...... way to make a bad situation go from disciplinary to a prosecutable federal crime, guys.
They're 80% of the reason the job is as difficult as it is, because they're loud AND dumb, but the loud is considered a virtue here so the dumb was tolerated. I'm not loud so I was considered underperforming when I'm actually half the reason the whole department runs. The other half is a senior member of the team who has no fucks to give because he's smarter and better rhan everyone. And he's a great guy.
I'm so done with this drama. I haven't seen shit like this since high school and I'm nearly 40. Between the "speak up more even if it's asinie" metric of performance that just mystifies me, and the blatant sexism/racism/stupidity that permeates every single team..... I'm glad to be going back to an industry with accountability and actual definable contract requirements. Everyone is openly antagonistic here. I've never worked a place where that was not only tolerated but lowkey encouraged. I'm probably a bit shocked that there's consequences for these guys at all. Trainee must be well-connected. Anyway, that's just me shouting into the void. Thanks for listening to my petty bullshit. Do me a couple favors: hug your loves ones, and make sure you're registered to vote. EDIT: The "Don't Vote" bot has visited here, and it can go fuck itself. GQP and Maga and Trumpers can all go fuck yourselves. Putin is a liar and a murderer. SLAVA UKRANI! Vote! Vote for your rights, your environment, your future, and your freedoms! No one will give them to you, you have to make them! Fight the oppressors who lie, who want you to be afraid of your fellows, remember who the real enemy is.
item.dc:creator: ViscousCousCous

This old, I'm this old. Loved Gilbert Gottfried in these as a kid.
Don't threaten me with a good time.

This one got me. I blew air quite loudly from my nose at 2am.

item.title: Goodbye mom
item.description:
This is my mom. She died recently after 7 years of struggling. She fought tooth and nail against it. Was willing to try everything. She went into the hospital for the last time in mid January because she became unresponsive at home and her respirations per minute was 10. She was in the hospital for 5 days. High potassium and ammonia. When that happens, people get delirious and act like they have dementia. While they were bringing that down, her oncologist came in to talk to me and told me that at this point, treating the cancer would kill her and make her miserable. There was nothing else he could do. Nothing else WE could do but make sure she was as comfortable and happy as possible until the end. I texted my aunties (her sisters) to let them know, I texted my sister (who lives in another state) to let her know. I texted her closest friends to let them know. Then, when the medication normalized her ammonia and potassium, we had to let my mom know. I had to tell my mom she was dying. Not just metaphorically this time. Before she got this bad, right after my dad died in September, I talked with her about what she wanted. At the time she said she didn’t want to do home hospice (which is what my dad did) because she didn’t want her grandson to see her like that. She was talking about my nephew who’s a high functioning autistic. She raised him because she saw the signs in him early on and was able to work with him and advocate for him in the school system, to get him the help he needed. She was a special needs teacher at one point in her life. It prepared her for taking care of my nephew. When we were told that there was no treatment left to try, that nephew made the decision to go to the hospital to see my mom there. My nephew, who avoids hospitals at all cost. My nephew, who probably was still traumatized after seeing my my dad go from hospital to home to dying, my nephew who previously said he didn’t want to go to the hospital because he couldn’t see his gramma that way, asked to go see her. When he arrived, he asked us to step out so he could talk to her alone. We did. When they were done talking, my mom asked to go home. Not to a hospice. Home.
My mom was the oldest in a family of 8, born and raised in Hawaii. She worked since she was 13, starting as a pineapple picker for Dole. As soon as she graduated high school, she tried to join the military… mostly because the recruiter told her they’d pay for her college. While the family wasn’t homeless or destitute, they were by no means well off enough to even think college was a possibility for them. She was still only 17 at the time, though, and had to convince her parents to sign off to let her join. Somehow she convinced them. She served in the Air Force, got shot at in Vietnam while identifying and tagging fallen soldiers in the field, when she got pregnant, her commanding officer made her work out of a small room at the back of the office so no one would notice she was out of uniform. Pregnant women in the military were medically discharged and she was pregnant with me. She left the Air Force then joined the Air National Guard where she stayed for almost 30 years. She retired just before she could get full retirement so that she could take care of her first grandbaby. But she never stopped working. She was a special needs teacher, a Hawaiian studies teacher, she created the schools garden where she taught the kids to tend plants. She’d make them plant seedlings, then replant them in the ground when they were stronger. She’s let them harvest what they grew when the crops were ready, and had them bring back seeds so the next year of students could do it all again. They called her Mama Aina. Mother Earth. She volunteered at her church to help them create a nature farm and sat on their board. Her two favorite places in the world to be was working in a garden and at the beach fishing. She would work from sun up to sun down and go home and work even more because my dad was a useless piece of shit that gambled away his money and gave her less than 1k a month to take care of mortgage, utilities, food, and other expenses. I didn’t realize how bad it was until now, going through her paperwork. But then she got sick.
She got really sick, really fast. The doctors scrambled to find out what was going on and they found out she was going through liver failure. My mom doesn’t drink and eats healthy so this was unexpected. The cause… chronic Hep B. Having been born before Hawaii was a state, they didn’t have vaccines then and Hep B was very common in the Filipino community. I moved in to help take care of her. Her husband wasn’t capable of doing it. She was on death’s door. Bad enough that they put her on the top of the transplant list and flew her out to Mayo in Arizona to wait. ‘It can take months or even years sometimes for a donor organ to come in’, they warned us. She wouldn’t have to wait long. It took less than 4 days for a viable donor to appear. They had just finished screening her to approve her for receiving the next donor, and it arrived. Less than a week after her transplant she was up and walking. 2 weeks post transplant and she was walking without a cane or walker. They cleared her go back home just shy of a month post-transplant. But they instructed her that she couldn’t do gardening or go into direct sunlight for a year. Thinking about it now, it must have made her feel so trapped. A year and a half after her chemo treatment, I was doing lomi lomi on her incision scars and notice something that didn’t feel like scar tissue. ‘Get it checked’, I told her. She did. The biopsy results showed ovarian cancer. OVARIAN CANCER just under the scar tissue of her torso. Right below her sternum. When they did a CT scan, there were small nodules in the abdominal fascia. Ovarian cancer. The news of cancer knocked us on our ass. They would never give a transplant to someone with a hint of it. They took out her ovaries but the cancer was in her abdominal wall. Too many nodules and too small to go in and cut them all out. Chemotherapy began. Just when she was about past the transplant restriction date to go to the beach and work in the garden, her chemo was strong and prevented her from going into the garden again. So 2020 wasn’t just a COVID year for us, it was a cancer year. I had to get more furniture and appliances for her. A fancy washer and dryer that could super sanitize things, a new stove since ours was old, rusted, and probably not as sanitary as it should be. This after I got her a new bed before she came home from her transplant, one that had rails and was higher off the ground, as well as ripped out all the carpets and refloored since, we were told, dust and things that could make her sick usually hid in rugs. She kept fighting. 4 different treatments, some of which slowed its progression but never really stopped it. One treatment seemed to bring her cancer numbers down and the scans of her abdomen looked like the nodules were shrinking… but cancer is a fucking asshole and it had moved to where the oncologist wasn’t looking. Where her liver doctor SHOULD HAVE been looking. It ended where it started. The cancer found its way into her liver. The doctor said that they tried to biopsy her liver but ‘there was no viable liver tissue’. It was all cancer.
This is my grandpa saying goodbye to my mom. Kids are supposed to say goodbye to their parents but parents shouldn’t have to say goodbye to their kids. But he had to. He did. I sometimes complained that I had to take care of her, that I used up all the money I saved for a down payment on a house on renovating hers and other expenses that came with taking care of her. Near the end I complained when she’d call me at 2am to come change her because she spoiled herself. Now I wake up at 2am out of habit… and wish I could go help her clean herself up. Now I wish I could take out another loan or max out another card to keep taking care of her. My mom was an amazing person for so many people. I’m realizing that now as I try to find all of the people that knew her and they tell me stories about her. She was selfless and gave everything of herself to take care of others. But that also put her in a bind financially. She took out her whole life insurance policies at one point to keep the house since it was going into foreclosure because, in Hawaii, her single income wasn’t enough to pay a mortgage, HOA fees, utilities, food, and other expenses. Yes, she was married, but her husband squandered his money on chicken fights and bars and cards. HIS money. They didn’t have a joint account. She didn’t trust him to have access to the money for the house, and he preferred to keep his money for himself. And now I get to the part I hate doing but I’m shameless enough to do so because I will do everything in my power to make sure she doesn’t lose this house. I need help. The house wasn’t in a trust so it’s going to probate where the creditors she owes money to is also going to go to collect what’s due. I need the help of a lawyer. On top of that, 2 funerals in 4 months is disgustingly expensive and my mom has no life insurance and no savings. I lost a great job due to long COVID and went from living well within my means to not even scraping by, and don’t have savings because I had used it up already. I just want to save the house so my nephew doesn’t have to leave this place he was raised. To keep it in the family, and know that her garden will be taken care of. https://gofund.me/f8bff23b Sorry for spam. I can talk forever about how amazing she is. Thank you for reading if you made it this far. Don’t be afraid to hug your loved ones and tell them you love them. Even when you know the ‘last time’ is coming, you’ll never be prepared. EDIT: Thank you all so much for your kind words, for your time to read about this amazing woman, and for your support. I know a lot of people are suspicious about these things. I watch too much drama and anti-MLM YouTube and would be wary as well. I’ll post updates about how the battle for the house goes, and about the services. My mom touched a LOT of people’s lives. One of them, ironically, runs a non profit that provides cancer survivors/patients support, resources, and a place to go to center themselves (https://iwalanifoundation.org/). I remember going with my mom decades ago to help prep this lady’s property/garden. Way before cancer was something on our radar. She wants to commemorate my mom’s memory by planting a tree there. I have an avocado tree my mom planted when we were in lockdown during COVID. I think it would be perfect for what she wants to do, and for the tree. Being in the ground will be a million times better than being in a pot. When family wakes, I have no doubt they’ll be blown away by the support and love that has come from you all. I know I already am. I can’t express enough how grateful I am.. even though I can be wordy, obviously.
item.dc:creator: Acquisitor

item.title: Was a bit drunk / high and had to excuse myself to the bathroom to almost die laughing.
item.description: I'm not a quiet laugher and I guess the walls were thin? As soon as I get back to my seat my mom whispers to me that "Everybody could hear you, it was almost a minute of very ugly laughing. I'm so embarrassed, nobody on my side laughs that way maybe your dad's." *front-page edit* Wow didn't expect this. Please be kind to one another even if you feel like they're undeserving of your kindness, some people are just having a bad day and the warmth you show them can go a long way.
item.dc:creator: BestofUwU

item.title: Australia, let's replace climate blockers with climate leaders!

item.title: Images of Note: plunk
item.description:
I would say he was fuckin' something but I'm not sure about the shoes.
This is not even the gayest thing you will see today
RIP AND TEAR!
It takes six months to train as a Cop in Florida
DUCK HELL! (Woohoo!)
that's just my typical day
Am Scottish, can confirm.
We can't all be Neurotypical Tiffany!
Imagine being an Air Crash investigator and the last thing you hear on the CVR is the captain playing the sax.
:
O-O
I want to inform you that the tumblr post I got this from had everyone calling the Possum a whore.
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay

item.title: I had the best mother and the best friend I'll ever have...
item.description: Multi-systemic organ failure as a result of CO2 poisoning, influenza A, pneumonia, and a staph infection of the blood. Pre-existing comorbidities were COPD and depressed kidney function. She was sick for a week and during that time her COPD prevented her from taking up enough oxygen and expelling enough carbon dioxide. It built up in her body until she collapsed on her couch. By the time my Dad found her in the early morning hours it was already too late. The damage was done and her body wasn't strong enough to repair itself. She was rushed to the ER and spent over a week in the ICU on ventilation, dialysis, and antibiotics. It was well known she had zero interest in living if it meant being hooked up to a machine unable to care for herself for the rest of her life. She passed peacefully under heavy medication not feeling any pain or stress. It's fucked up. It really is... At least she got to live long enough to see her son quit drinking for good...even if I couldn't repair the damage I did to my life to become successful before she passed, she still got to see her son defeat a 13 year long demon...
item.dc:creator: JustSomeDoodWhoWantsToUseThisWebsiteToInteractWithFellowPeople

item.title: Just wanted to point on there is only $70 dollars in bills on the table
item.description: 2 $20 1 $10 1 $5 15 $1 40+10+5+15=70 And I am only seeing two baggies of marijuana. Did they raid a middle schooler? What's in the prescription bottle - Ritalin? Looks like someone ratted out their older brother. Tax dollars well spent.

item.title: For Gondor! A Messenger bag I recently made
